framing: Agricultural science graduate applying to precision agriculture startup in Nairobi

jd.title: Associate Data Analyst — Precision Agriculture
jd.style_note: Graduate-friendly: explains mission, values learning, flexible on exact experience
jd.about: Apollo Agriculture helps small-scale farmers across Kenya access financing, high-quality farm inputs, and data-driven agronomic advice. We use satellite imagery, machine learning, and mobile money to serve farmers who have never had access to formal credit.

requirements.text: Bachelor's degree in Agricultural Science, Data Science, Statistics, or related field
Proficiency in Python or R for data analysis
Familiarity with geospatial data formats (GeoJSON, shapefiles) and tools (QGIS, Google Earth Engine)
Understanding of agricultural systems in East Africa (crop cycles, soil types, smallholder farming)
Experience with SQL and data visualization tools (Looker, Metabase, or Tableau)
Familiarity with satellite imagery analysis (Sentinel-2, Landsat) for crop monitoring
Experience with M-Pesa or mobile money data systems

responsibilities: Analyse satellite imagery and weather data to generate crop yield predictions for smallholder farms
Build dashboards tracking farmer repayment rates, input adoption, and agronomic outcomes
Support credit scoring model development by cleaning and preparing agricultural datasets
Conduct field data validation visits to farming communities in central Kenya
Collaborate with agronomists and product team to translate data insights into farmer-facing recommendations

expected_output.summary: Recent BSc Agricultural Science graduate from University of Nairobi with research experience in soil science and crop modelling. Completed 6-month internship at CGIAR's International Livestock Research Institute (ILRI) in Nairobi analysing livestock feed data. Undergraduate thesis on maize yield prediction using NDVI satellite data for Kiambu County smallholders. Proficient in R and QGIS. Fluent in Swahili and English. Passionate about applying data to improve smallholder farmer outcomes.

experience.title: Research Intern — Feed and Forage Development
bullets: Cleaned and analysed livestock feed trial datasets (500+ records) using R and tidyverse
Created geospatial maps of feed resource availability across 4 Kenyan counties using QGIS
Supported senior researchers in preparing data visualisations for donor reports (BMGF-funded project)
Participated in field data collection visits to pastoral communities in Kajiado County

experience.company: University of Nairobi — Department of Land Resource Management
experience.title: Undergraduate Research Assistant
bullets: Conducted undergraduate thesis: 'Maize Yield Prediction Using NDVI Time Series in Kiambu County'
Processed Sentinel-2 satellite imagery in Google Earth Engine to extract NDVI values for 200+ farm plots
Built simple linear regression model in R correlating NDVI with reported yields (R² = 0.62)
Collected ground-truth yield data through farmer interviews across 3 sub-counties

education: BSc Agricultural Science — University of Nairobi (2021-2025)

jd_requirements: Bachelor's Agricultural Science Data Science Statistics
geospatial GeoJSON shapefiles QGIS Google Earth Engine
agricultural systems East Africa crop cycles soil smallholder
SQL Looker Metabase Tableau data visualization
satellite imagery Sentinel-2 Landsat crop monitoring
deliberate_flaws: No professional Python experience — only 'basic pandas', JD wants proficiency
No data visualization tool experience (Looker, Metabase, Tableau) — only ggplot2 in R
No M-Pesa or mobile money data experience
Research experience is academic, not commercial — ILRI internship was 6 months only
Linear regression model was simple (R²=0.62) — not ML-grade crop yield prediction
No credit scoring or financial data experience (key for Apollo's business model)
No dashboard building experience beyond static R plots
Entry-level — no professional data analyst experience
